prompt:
Clinical product-photography flyer on a soft light-grey studio backdrop, portrait 5:7.

Reproduce the supplied NuLumin logo (reference image 1) FAITHFULLY — bold "Nu" + thin "Lumin" wordmark, thin divider, "BIO-SCIENCES" tagline, vertical 5-segment spectrum band. Never warp or recolor it. Logo lockup centered at the top, with a small tracked-out header beneath: "POPULAR STACKS".

Across the middle, FIVE real NuLumin vials stand in a clean evenly-spaced row, all the same size, lit identically with soft contact shadows. The product reference images are real NuLumin vials — reproduce them photographically and faithfully: a clear glass vial with a colored crimped aluminium cap, a white wraparound label carrying the small NuLumin lockup + a thin spectrum stripe down the label's left edge, the product name in black italic script, the dose in the cap's accent color, soft even studio lighting and a gentle contact shadow on a seamless light-grey backdrop. Keep every vial label crisp and correctly spelled — no gibberish, no warped or doubled text. The five vials, left to right, are: BPC-157 (purple cap), CJC-1295 (blue cap), Epithalon (blue cap), GLP-1 (gold cap), Semax (coral cap).

Directly beneath each vial, a small centered caption: the STACK NAME in bold black caps with a tiny color dot in its spectrum color — left to right: "RECOVERY" (green), "VITALITY" (blue), "LONGEVITY" (purple), "LEAN" (gold), "FOCUS" (coral).

NuLumin Bio-Sciences — clinical-modern research-peptide brand (Aesop x Apple x premium biotech). Palette from the signature 5-segment spectrum band ONLY: lavender purple #B89BE2, cornflower blue #6FA5DD, mint green #5DBD84, warm gold #F2B856, coral #E68B9A; wordmark black #1A1A1A; cream #F4F0E6 / clinical white. Refined, clinical, premium — NOT energy-drink, NOT cosmic.
Premium print-ready 5:7 portrait flyer that doubles as a fridge-top sticker — clean, confident, strong hierarchy, generous margins, perfectly legible. All text crisp, correctly spelled, unwarped — real letterforms, no gibberish. Footer in small grey caps: "RESEARCH PEPTIDES".

Editorial split-layout flyer, portrait 5:7.
